name: Philodendron verrucosum
aboutText: Philodendron verrucosum is a stunning hemiepiphytic aroid native to the wet tropical forests stretching from Costa Rica through to Peru. It is highly prized by collectors for its iconic broad, heart-shaped leaves that possess a striking, deep-green velvety texture and prominent pale venation. The species is most easily identified by its distinctive densely bristled or scaly petioles, which add a unique tactile element to its appearance. As a climbing aroid, it thrives in warm, humid understory conditions, typically ascending trees in its natural habitat using specialized aerial roots.

fieldNotes.title: Warted and Velvety from the Andes
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Philodendron verrucosum is named for the warty petioles covered in distinctive glandular trichomes that catch light and give the stems a rough, bristled quality. The leaves are velvety — soft, light-catching, with the subtle sheen of the velvet Philodendron group — and produced on dramatically hairy petioles. From Costa Rica to Peru along the Andean foothills. At accessible price tier, which is an undervaluation: a velvety-leaved Philodendron with dramatic petioles at single-tier pricing is good value by any measure. Grow it with the humidity it wants.
